summary: Lead started M6-MAP-CANDIDATE-PIPELINE-001 after M6 scenario data pipeline closure entered origin/main. client_engineer writer thread 019f07f7-2cfb-7753-b583-283d8542501b is assigned to implement the complete Alpha map candidate pipeline path within task allowed paths.

tests_run.result: M6-MAP-CANDIDATE-PIPELINE-001 and M6-SUCCESSION-COMPLETION-001 were dependency-ready after scenario data closure.
Spawned client_engineer writer thread 019f07f7-2cfb-7753-b583-283d8542501b, nickname Window.

decisions: Start M6-MAP-CANDIDATE-PIPELINE-001 as the first taskctl READY M6 task after M6-SCENARIO-DATA-PIPELINE-001 closure.
Keep this as an Alpha map candidate pipeline, not M7 final map/content lock.
Map renderer changes must remain presentational/read-model oriented and must not become authoritative simulation state or hidden sim input.
Do not touch unrelated untracked ui-samples/.
risks: R3 task crosses content runtime/schema/tools and map-renderer read-model boundaries; systems_architect review is required before acceptance.
Historical uncertainty must remain visible through source labels and review notes; exact borders and cultural symbols are not locked in this task.
requested_action: Implement the complete Alpha map candidate pipeline path within allowed paths, run required tests, and return a client_engineer REVIEW handoff for systems_architect review.

acceptance_criteria: A complete Alpha map candidate path exists from validated source data through deterministic compiled map payloads to map-renderer-compatible read models.
Map candidate IDs, district references, route references, water/land classifications, and rendering order are stable and explicitly schema-validated.
Map renderer changes remain presentational and do not become authoritative state, hidden simulation input, or React-managed per-entity Pixi object ownership.
The task does not final-lock exact borders, production art style, formal cultural symbols, complex naval warfare, 3D battle, manual node battle, or release map content.
required_tests: Map candidate tests cover deterministic ordering, bad references, route/water classification, renderer read-model shape, and no sim-core authority import.
